  Scenario: Converting a POJO with non-String fields to Properties
    Given a POJO with an Integer field "A" having value 129
    And a String field "B" having value "13"
    When the POJO is converted to an instance of "java.util.Properties"
    Then the "Properties" instance should contain key "A" with String value "129"

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<!-- ====================================================================== -->
<!-- Ant build file (http://ant.apache.org/) for Ant 1.6.2 or above.        -->
<!-- ====================================================================== -->

<project name="jackson-databind" default="package" basedir=".">

  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <!-- Import maven-build.xml into the current project                        -->
  <!-- ====================================================================== -->

  <import file="maven-build.xml"/>

  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <!-- Help target                                                            -->
  <!-- ====================================================================== -->

  <target name="help">
    <echo message="Please run: $ant -projecthelp"/>
  </target>

  <target name="compile" depends="jackson-databind-from-maven.compile"> </target>

  <target name="compile.tests" depends="jackson-databind-from-maven.compile,jackson-databind-from-maven.compile-tests-custom"> </target>

  <!-- Custom test compilation that includes cucumber dependencies -->
  <target name="jackson-databind-from-maven.compile-tests-custom" 
          depends="jackson-databind-from-maven.compile"
          description="Compile the test code with cucumber dependencies"
          unless="maven.test.skip">
    <mkdir dir="${maven.build.testOutputDir}"/>
    <javac destdir="${maven.build.testOutputDir}"
           nowarn="true"
           debug="true"
           optimize="true"
           deprecation="true"
           target="1.6"
           verbose="false"
           fork="false"
           source="1.6">
      <src>
        <pathelement location="${maven.build.testDir.0}"/>
      </src>
      <classpath>
        <path refid="build.test.classpath"/>
        <pathelement location="${maven.build.outputDir}"/>
        <pathelement path="${d4j.tests.extras}"/>
      </classpath>
    </javac>
    <copy todir="${maven.build.testOutputDir}">
      <fileset dir="${maven.build.testResourceDir.0}"/>
    </copy>
  </target>

  <!-- Override test execution to include cucumber dependencies -->
  <target name="test" depends="compile-tests, junit-missing" unless="junit.skipped">
    <mkdir dir="${maven.test.reports}"/>
    <junit printSummary="yes" haltonerror="true" haltonfailure="true" fork="true" dir=".">
      <sysproperty key="basedir" value="."/>
      <formatter type="xml"/>
      <formatter type="plain" usefile="false"/>
      <classpath>
        <path refid="build.test.classpath"/>
        <pathelement location="${maven.build.outputDir}"/>
        <pathelement location="${maven.build.testOutputDir}"/>
        <pathelement path="${d4j.tests.extras}"/>
      </classpath>
      <batchtest todir="${maven.test.reports}" unless="test">
        <fileset dir="${maven.build.testDir.0}">
          <include name="**/Test*.java"/>
          <include name="**/*Test.java"/>
          <include name="**/*TestCase.java"/>
          <exclude name="com/fasterxml/jackson/failing/*.java"/>
        </fileset>
      </batchtest>
      <batchtest todir="${maven.test.reports}" if="test">
        <fileset dir="${maven.build.testDir.0}">
          <include name="**/${test}.java"/>
          <exclude name="com/fasterxml/jackson/failing/*.java"/>
        </fileset>
      </batchtest>
    </junit>
  </target>

</project>
EOL
